quickFacts: The tower leans about 4 degrees — and it's stabilised now
Pisa is a university town — the student area has the best cheap eats
It's a great base for day trips to Florence, Lucca, and Cinque Terre
Aperitivo hour means free snacks with your drink
foodScene: Cecina (chickpea flatbread) is the local street food king. Try torta co' bischeri (a sweet rice tart). Student-friendly trattorias around Piazza delle Vettovaglie serve huge pasta portions for under €8. Gelato from Bottega del Gelato near the river.

places.name: Leaning Tower
places.caption: The ultimate tilt
places.description: Yes, do the cliché photo. But also climb the 294 steps — the lean makes the spiral staircase a surreal experience. Book timed tickets online.

places.name: Piazza dei Miracoli
places.caption: Square of Miracles
places.description: The Duomo, Baptistery, and Camposanto together on emerald-green grass. The Baptistery's acoustics are mind-blowing — wait for the guard's demo.

places.name: Borgo Stretto
places.caption: Arcaded shopping street
places.description: Covered medieval walkways with local shops, cafés, and gelaterias. The real Pisa lives here, not at the tower. Great for an evening stroll.

places.name: Lungarno (River Arno Banks)
places.caption: Sunset river walk
places.description: Colourful palazzi reflected in the Arno at golden hour. The Luminara festival in June lines the banks with 70,000 candles.

places.name: Piazza delle Vettovaglie
places.caption: Market square by day, bars by night
places.description: Morning fresh market, afternoon aperitivo, late-night student hangout. The most authentic piazza in Pisa.

places.name: Keith Haring Mural
places.caption: Tuttomondo street art
places.description: Keith Haring painted this massive mural on a church wall in 1989. It was his last public work and it's completely free to see.

places.name: Orto Botanico
places.caption: Europe's oldest botanical garden
places.description: Founded in 1544, this peaceful garden is a hidden gem. Centuries-old trees, a tiny museum, and almost no tourists.

places.name: Camposanto Monumentale
places.caption: Medieval frescoed cloister
places.description: A stunning marble cloister said to contain soil from the Holy Land. The Triumph of Death frescoes are hauntingly beautiful.

places.name: Day Trip: Lucca
places.caption: Walled town by bike
places.description: Only 30 minutes by train. Rent a bike and ride atop the Renaissance walls, explore the oval piazza built inside a Roman amphitheatre.

places.name: Day Trip: Cinque Terre
places.caption: Colourful coastal villages
places.description: Five pastel-painted villages clinging to cliffs. Take the train from Pisa (1h 15min) and hike between towns along the coast.

places.name: Leaning Tower & Piazza dei Miracoli
places.caption: The famous tilt
places.description: Climb 294 steps up the 56m tower (leaning 4° since the 12th century). The Campo is stunning — Cathedral, Baptistery and Camposanto.

places.name: Piazza dei Cavalieri
places.caption: Knights' square
places.description: Pisa's second-most-important square with lavish Palazzo della Carovana (now the prestigious Scuola Normale Superiore).

places.name: Battistero di San Giovanni
places.caption: Baptistery
places.description: Largest baptistery in Italy. The acoustics are legendary — guards demonstrate the echo every 30 minutes. Combine with Tower ticket.

places.name: Lungarno Walks
places.caption: Arno riverside
places.description: Walk along the Arno riverbanks past colourful buildings, the Gothic church of Santa Maria della Spina and sunset reflections.

places.name: San Rossore Natural Park
places.caption: Coastal nature
places.description: Pine forest, sand dunes and beaches on the coast. Horse riding, cycling and birdwatching. A green escape from the tourist crowds.

places.name: Cinque Terre Day Trip
places.caption: Five colourful villages
places.description: 1-hour train to five cliff-hanging villages connected by hiking trails. Swim, eat pesto and drink Sciacchetrà wine.

places.name: Lucca Day Trip
places.caption: Walled city by bike
places.description: 30 min by train. Cycle the 4 km city walls, climb Torre Guinigi (trees on top), and eat buccellato cake. Charming and quiet.
